Bivariate Explorer

Yopougon Millionnaire-Extension — April/May 2026

Author

YopouLab consortium

Published

June 25, 2026

Bivariate Explorer

Explore pairwise associations across three units of analysis: cluster (n = 32 spatial units), household (n = 1,495), and individual (sub-sample, n = 1,981). Correlations are Spearman rank-based and computed with pairwise deletion of missing values. Significance thresholds are unadjusted and intended for exploration, not inference.

  • Correlation matrices
  • Bivariate scatter

Cells show the Spearman ρ coefficient; significance is indicated by stars (\* p < 0.05, \\ p < 0.01, \\\* p < 0.001). Hover over any cell for the exact ρ, p-value, and pairwise n. The diagonal is left blank (perfect self-correlation, ρ = 1). Cells with insufficient pairwise data (n < 10) are shown as n/a.

  • Cluster (n = 32)
  • Household (n = 1,495)
  • Individual (n = 1,981)

Select any two variables on the X and Y axes. The central panel shows a scatterplot coloured by stratum with 2D kernel density contours in the background. The top and right strips show per-stratum 1D KDE curves aligned to the central axes. Points with missing values on either selected variable are excluded (pairwise deletion). Click a stratum name in the legend to toggle all its traces.

  • Cluster (n = 32)
  • Household (n = 1,495)
  • Individual (n = 1,981)
X axis Y axis
X axis Y axis
X axis Y axis

For continuous/ordinal × continuous/ordinal pairs: Spearman ρ with pairwise deletion. For binary × continuous/ordinal pairs: rank-biserial correlation rrb (= Spearman ρ applied to a binary variable) displayed inline; unadjusted logistic OR shown as supplementary detail. The survey was powered with a design effect of 1.5 (cluster sampling), so estimates are stable, but standard errors from the logistic regression do not account for the cluster structure and 95% CIs may be slightly anti-conservative. No correction for multiple comparisons. YopouLab consortium / Swiss TPH & CSRS — 2026.
